Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Monuses and Heaps 34 upvotes, 2 comments
  2. Project Nomad – Knowledge That Never Goes Offline 127 upvotes, 20 comments
  3. My first patch to the Linux kernel 171 upvotes, 31 comments
  4. A Review of Dice That Came with the White Castle 66 upvotes, 10 comments
  5. Building an FPGA 3dfx Voodoo with Modern RTL Tools 70 upvotes, 12 comments
  6. Some things just take time 788 upvotes, 250 comments
  7. The IBM scientist who rewrote the rules of information just won a Turing Award 25 upvotes, 3 comments
  8. 25 Years of Eggs 156 upvotes, 47 comments
  9. I Hate: Programming Wayland Applications 55 upvotes, 13 comments
  10. HopTab–free,open source macOS app switcher and tiler that replaces Cmd+Tab 84 upvotes, 23 comments
  11. A Fuzzer for the Toy Optimizer 25 upvotes, 4 comments
  12. Professional video editing, right in the browser with WebGPU and WASM 324 upvotes, 118 comments
  13. Flash-Moe: Running a 397B Parameter Model on a Mac with 48GB RAM 179 upvotes, 61 comments
  14. A Coherent Vision for the Future of Version Control 12 upvotes, 1 comments
  15. More common mistakes to avoid when creating system architecture diagrams 60 upvotes, 21 comments
  16. Tinybox – A powerful computer for deep learning 550 upvotes, 319 comments
  17. The three pillars of JavaScript bloat 407 upvotes, 239 comments
  18. Chest Fridge (2009) 160 upvotes, 87 comments
  19. Brute-Forcing My Algorithmic Ignorance with an LLM in 7 Days 46 upvotes, 20 comments
  20. Node.js worker threads are problematic, but they work great for us 39 upvotes, 18 comments
  21. Cloudflare flags archive.today as "C&C/Botnet"; no longer resolves via 1.1.1.2 222 upvotes, 182 comments
  22. Atlassian says it had right to fire engineer for suggesting CEO is 'rich jerk' 80 upvotes, 57 comments
  23. Why Lab Coats Turned White 25 upvotes, 13 comments
  24. iBook Clamshell 34 upvotes, 24 comments
  25. Apple's intentional crippling of Mobile Safari 93 upvotes, 88 comments
  26. Windows native app development is a mess 99 upvotes, 99 comments
  27. $ teebot.dev – from terminal to tee in 6 seconds 26 upvotes, 29 comments
  28. A case against currying 44 upvotes, 59 comments
  29. How We Synchronized Editing for Rec Room's Multiplayer Scripting System 12 upvotes, 11 comments
